methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

data.narrative:
Tubulis is a Series C biotech company that has raised €436M+ to date. The peer-median method anchors on a three-company cohort of same-stage, same-sector private companies: Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B), representing the broader universe of 8 private and 3 public comparable businesses in Biotech & Health.

The peer-median approach yields $2.4B, with the three comparables spanning a $1.5B to $4.0B range. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales was not applicable; while the Biotech & Health public cohort (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) trades at a 5.8x median multiple, Tubulis does not publicly disclose revenue, preventing reliable multiple application. Consequently, only one triangulation method could be executed.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.8B, centered on the peer median of $2.4B. This range reflects ±15% variance from the central estimate. Key uncertainties include reliance on a single method, limited peer visibility into Tubulis's pipeline maturity relative to comparables, and the inherent dispersion across the peer set ($1.5B–$4.0B), which suggests heterogeneity in stage substratification or commercial risk profiles not captured in stage classification alone. Revenue disclosure would enable public-multiple triangulation and improve confidence.
